rationale: Review 2 correctly identifies that `unipotent_radical_trivial : True`, `radical_trivial : True`, and `unipotentRadical_trivial'` all carry names that advertise substantive mathematical content while their types are just `True` — verified directly in the diff at lines 61-66. Acting on Review 2 would produce a PR where names match their actual statements (either by removing placeholders or renaming them honestly), which is the core of the naming rubric; Review 1 leaves these misleading names merged.
